mnemonics: SCHEMA: Structure Creates Hierarchy, Enabling Management Access
INSTANCE: Individual New Sets of Tables, Attributes, and New Column Entries
keyPoints: Three Schema Architecture: Internal Schema (physical storage), Conceptual Schema (logical structure), External Schema (user views)
Data Independence: Logical (changes in conceptual schema don't affect external) and Physical (changes in internal schema don't affect conceptual)
Instances are the actual data stored in the database at a particular moment
commonMistakes: Assuming changes to physical storage automatically affect how users view data
Forgetting that logical data independence allows changing conceptual schema without rewriting application programs
